etymology.description: Originally 隹 (bird) -> added 唯 (phonetic) and 口 (mouth) to signify bird sound -> currently phonetic 唯 + semantic 鳥 (bird) = bird's cry -> cry of a bird
etymology.interpretation: 鷕(yǎo, "cry of a bird") combines 唯(wéi, "only") for its phonetic properties, indicating sound, with 鳥(niǎo, "bird"), representing its semantic meaning of a bird's cry. This illustrates a phonetic addition to represent the sound aspect ('cry') while retaining the bird concept, leading to the meaning 'cry of a bird'.
